title: Opus Audio
documentation:
## Purpose
Represents an Opus-encoded audio file, an open and patent-free codec designed for low-latency speech and music transmission over networks. Opus delivers excellent quality across a wide range of bitrates and is the recommended codec for WebRTC and internet audio streaming.

## When to use
Use `media:opus;audio` when a node produces or consumes Opus audio, such as voice communication recordings, WebRTC captures, or streaming audio pipeline outputs. For general compressed audio, see [OGG Audio](https://capdag.com/browse/media:ogg;audio) or [AAC Audio](https://capdag.com/browse/media:aac;audio).

## Notes
Opus files are typically wrapped in an Ogg container (`.opus` extension). The codec is optimised for low latency and handles both speech and music well. Whisper and similar transcription models can accept Opus audio directly in many implementations.
